title: Severe Acute Malnutrition Wasting Detection And Treatment Coverage In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying where severe acute malnutrition wasting prevalence is highest and treatment coverage is lowest in Sub-Saharan Africa, linking nutrition survey data, health facility records, and community-based management programs at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: Severe acute malnutrition kills over 500,000 children under five annually. Ready-to-use therapeutic foods and community-based management can treat 90 percent of cases if children are reached. But treatment coverage in Sub-Saharan Africa averages below 30 percent, and the gap between SAM prevalence surveys and treatment program reach is rarely systematically verified at district scale.

success_metrics.target: Every district SAM burden estimate and treatment coverage estimate links to dated survey or program data with methodology.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against evidence.json and evidence.md.

success_metrics.target: No nutrition program or supply-chain recommendation merged without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status, review records, and safety flags.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review for program claims, replication for quantitative analyses.
